mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off portfolio rebalancing that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling real consumer credit indicates a structural shift in demand composition where housing absorbs liquidity without broadening consumption, compressing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST up, RSAFS down) fails to predict positive SPX returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above trend during a late-cycle expansion phase boosts corporate profit margins through pricing power and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds 102 for three consecutive months, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 during peak expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to suppress equity multiples and trigger valuation compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 2.5% for two consecutive quarters, or the forward return distribution shows no statistical difference from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic resilience and supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative SPX returns in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS grows above 150,000 for three consecutive months, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real GDP growth and consumer credit outstanding signal a liquidity crunch that restricts corporate working capital and consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite concurrent GDP and credit declines, or the joint signal's predictive accuracy falls below chance levels when tested against a random baseline over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 98 during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that erode corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO stays below 98 for two consecutive months, or the signal's directional accuracy does not exceed 55% in a walk-forward test spanning multiple recovery cycles.
